title: Bruins’ Charlie McAvoy scores winning goal. And then it was time to see the dentist — again.
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the winning goal 39 seconds into overtime as Bruins beat Kings 2-1.
McAvoy, suffering from dental injuries, visited a dentist after the game despite pain from a puck hit.
Bruins coach praised McAvoy's resilience, hinting his toughness could lead to a leadership role.

title: Jaylen Brown blasts refs on social media after being ejected from Spurs game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected after receiving two technical fouls in Celtics' game against the Spurs.
Brown criticized referees on social media shortly after his ejection, expressing frustration over officiating.
The Celtics, missing key players, lost 125-116 to Spurs; Brown's absence was notable after his ejection.

title: Man sentenced for fatally stabbing Chelsea woman, 59
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Angel Alvarez, 68, sentenced to life in prison with parole eligibility after 17 years for the murder of Margarita Morehead.
Alvarez pleaded guilty to second-degree murder, assault and battery, and violating a protection order in Chelsea stabbing case.
Despite a restraining order, Alvarez remained in contact with Morehead and fatally stabbed her during an argument in May 2023.

title: MBTA police seeking suspect who allegedly spat on, hit rider
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The alleged assault occurred on February 27 at 8 p.m. in the Prudential MBTA station.
The suspect allegedly spat on the victim and hit them multiple times with a closed fist.
Transit Police shared images of the suspect and request public assistance for identification.

title: Patriots reportedly remain interested in A.J. Brown trade after signing Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $80 million contract to enhance their receiver corps.
Despite signing Doubs, Patriots remain interested in trading for Eagles' wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Eagles reportedly seek a first-round and a second-round pick for A.J. Brown, but New England may wait for better terms.

title: She’s running the Boston Marathon to support breast cancer patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sydney Weisman, 24, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Team Ellie to support breast cancer patients.
Weisman was inspired by her mother's battle with breast cancer and the support provided by the Ellie Fund.
The Ellie Fund provides transportation, meals, childcare, and daily support to breast cancer patients and their families.

title: 3 Stop & Shop locations in Boston, Reading receive bomb threats, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Bomb threats were reported at Stop & Shop locations in Jamaica Plain, Allston, and Reading on Monday afternoon.
Police responded to threats within minutes of each other, evacuating stores and conducting searches with bomb squads.
No explosives were found at the locations, but police are investigating if the threats are connected.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots WR Romeo Doubs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed wide receiver Romeo Doubs to a four-year, $68 million contract, with a maximum value of $80 million.
Doubs recorded 55 catches for 724 yards and six touchdowns for the Packers in 2025, showcasing his value in short-yardage situations.
Doubs, known for his playoff performances, posted 20 catches for 371 yards and two touchdowns in four career postseason games.

title: Southbridge woman dies after being rescued from house fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A woman in her 60s died after being rescued from a house fire at 417 South St., Southbridge.
She was initially taken to UMass Memorial-Harington Hospital and later died at Mass General Hospital.
The fire had no audible alarms, marking the 8th Massachusetts home fire death this year in homes lacking working smoke alarms.

title: New ‘call a boomer’ phone line connects young people in Boston to seniors in Nevada
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Matter Neuroscience launched 'call a boomer' phone line to connect Boston youth with Nevada seniors.
The service operates between Boston University campus and Sierra Manor in Reno and is free to use.
Conversations aim to bridge generational gaps and will be recorded for potential use on social media.
